meta.title: Dynamic Weather Risk Assessment
meta.subtitle: {site} / {activity} — weather working limits
meta.author: HSE Renewables
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label; site / turbine / threshold data are asset data, not PII. Any prior weather-incident context was de-identified before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This dynamic weather risk assessment sets the point-of-work weather working limits for {activity} at {site}. Each weather parameter is led by a NAMED NUMERIC threshold -> a hold / stop / evacuate action -> a mandatory re-assessment trigger. Wind is measured at hub height, not base of tower. The BS 7121-1 (2016) 7 m/s man-riding ceiling is the verified anchor; the hub-height cut-off ~= 15 m/s is [ASSUMED — proposed-and-user-confirmed]; every other threshold is user-confirmed or [GAP].

A weather control with no number is rejected. Every threshold names a value, a hold/stop/evacuate action and a re-assessment trigger. 'Monitor the wind and stop if it gets too windy' is NOT a defensible control.
Weather working limits — threshold -> action -> re-assessment

rows: Stand down and evacuate exposed positions
Hold or stop per the trigger
sections.caption: Each parameter: a named numeric threshold (with its measurement point) -> a pre-decided action -> a mandatory re-assessment trigger

rows.title: {finding title}
rows.description: {description traced to evidence — e.g. an activity lacked a numeric wind stop limit, or a base-of-tower reading was used for a tower-top decision}

rows.control: Reschedule weather-sensitive work to a forecast low-weather window (eliminate the exposure)
Real-time hub-height anemometer with an automated stop alert at the threshold
Lightning-detection stand-down procedure + the published weather working-limits matrix in every permit

rows.action: {SMART action closing a [GAP] — e.g. confirm the hub-height wind cut-off with the turbine OEM in-service limit}

items: Work at Height Regulations 2005 (SI 2005/735) reg 4 / 6 / 7 — weather governs whether work at height proceeds
BS 7121-1 (2016 edition) — man-riding wind ceiling 16 mph / 7 m/s [VERIFIED anchor]
NFPA 780 — lightning stand-down practice (detection radius + time user/service-confirmed)
Hub-height wind cut-off ~= 15 m/s [ASSUMED A4 — industry baseline, proposed-and-user-confirmed, not a fixed standard]
